name: OpenBindings Context Store
description: Interface for storing and retrieving context keyed by stable identifiers (typically a normalized API origin). Context is opaque per-target runtime data — credentials, session state, consent flags, custom headers, environment variables, metadata, or any other invoker-managed state. This role models the minimum surface a runtime needs: fetch the context for an invocation, persist newly-resolved context, and remove it on logout. Implementations MAY expose richer management capabilities (listing, inspection, rotation, audit) outside this role contract; that surface is implementation-defined and not part of the role.

Context.description: Opaque per-target context payload. Well-known field conventions used across OpenBindings drivers — credential fields: 'bearerToken' (string) for bearer auth, 'apiKey' (string) for API-key auth, 'basic' (object with 'username' and 'password') for HTTP basic auth, 'accessToken' / 'refreshToken' / 'expiresAt' for OAuth; transport fields: 'headers' / 'cookies' / 'environment' (objects with string values) and 'metadata' (object with arbitrary values) for per-target HTTP headers, cookies, environment variables, and invoker-specific metadata. Credentials are one kind of context, not the whole concept — implementations and callers MAY add additional fields for session state, consent flags, custom configuration, or any invoker-managed runtime data. The store SHOULD treat the payload as opaque.

getContext.description: Retrieve the context stored for the given key. Returns null when no context exists for the key.

key.description: Context store key (typically a normalized API origin).

setContext.description: Create or replace the context for the given key. The supplied context fully replaces any existing context.

deleteContext.description: Remove the context for the given key. Deleting a key that does not exist MUST succeed without error.
